stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Musicians around the world have used protest songs to call attention to environmental injustices.
  • Brazilian singer Gilberto Gil released the protest song “Refazenda” in 1975.
  • It celebrated traditional Bahian farming practices threatened by industrial agriculture in northeast Brazil.
  • Australian singer-songwriter Archie Roach released the protest song “Took the Children Away” in 1990.
  • It exposed the forced removal of Indigenous Australian children from their families during the twentieth century.
stem: The student wants to contrast the song “Refazenda” with the song “Took the Children Away.” Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: While both are protest songs, “Refazenda” is about traditional Bahian farming threatened by industrial agriculture, whereas “Took the Children Away” is about the forced removal of Indigenous Australian children.
The songs “Refazenda” and “Took the Children Away” both drew attention to social and environmental injustices.
Archie Roach’s song “Took the Children Away,” released in 1990, exposed the forced removal of Indigenous Australian children from their families.
Released in 1975 by Gilberto Gil, the song “Refazenda” celebrated traditional Bahian farming practices threatened by industrial agriculture.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. It names both songs and articulates the contrast: each addresses a different subject (Bahian farming vs. forced child removal).

Choice B states a shared theme rather than a contrast. Choices C and D describe only one of the two songs.
